subject: English

lesson.story_mission: Follow the Story Trail to turn small moments into clear narratives.
lesson.objective: I can tell a short event in order, add details, and write an ending.
lesson.concept_explanation: A narrative tells what happened. Writers introduce the event, place actions in an order readers can follow, add details about actions, thoughts, or feelings, and finish with an ending.
lesson.common_misconception: A list of related actions is not always a clear story; readers also need a sensible order, specific details, and an ending.
lesson.real_world_connection: We use narratives to share true experiences and imagined adventures with family, friends, and classmates.
lesson.lesson_summary: Tell who and where, order the events, add exact details, and end the small moment.
lesson.mini_lesson: Picture the moment. Ask what happened first, next, and last. Add one detail that helps the reader imagine it, then reread the ending.

worked_examples.text: Mia writes: “Yesterday, I learned to jump rope. First, Dad held one end of the rope. Next, I jumped over it three times. At last, I could do it alone, and I grinned.”
worked_examples.correct_answer: The actions are in order and the final sentence tells how Mia felt.

worked_examples.text: Revision: Change “We did stuff at the pond” to “We dipped our nets into the cool pond and found a tiny tadpole.”
worked_examples.correct_answer: The revision names exact actions and adds details a reader can picture.

hint_ladder: Find the part of the story the question asks about.
Use the sequence, detail, or ending clue to make your choice.
Reread the whole story and check that it is clear and complete.

guided_practice.prompt: Which sequence word best shows that the seed was pressed into the soil after the pot was filled?
hints: Locate this event: “I pressed one striped seed into the soil.”
Ask what happened immediately before and after that event in the planting a sunflower story.
Insert your word and reread all three events to be sure time moves forward.
guided_practice.explanation: “Next” shows when “I pressed one striped seed into the soil.” happens in relation to the other two events.
guided_practice.feedback: Strong story thinking—your choice helps a reader follow and picture the event.

question_audio.label: Read Question
